title: Eleutherococcus nanpingensis
intro: Eleutherococcus nanpingensis, a species within the Eleutherococcus genus, is a plant classified as a type of adaptogenic shrub. It has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate stress, improve cognitive function, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Eleutherococcus nanpingensis include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and extracts, often in combination with other adaptogenic herbs like ginseng and ashwagandha.
taxonomy: Eleutherococcus nanpingensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It is native to China and was first described in 2004.
